title: De novo alignments of RNA-seq data for human cell lines, now unavailable, to assess the KDM6A loss reported by Lee and colleagues (PMID 36638328)
author: Derti, Adnan
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Introduction 
 This dataset contains genomic alignments and derived results generated  de novo  from RNA-seq data that accompanied an article and was made public by the authors. The motivation for these alignments was to examine claims in the article much more directly than is feasible with standard expression results. 
 Many RNA-seq data sets deposited in the same archive as these reads are diligently and systematically re-analyzed by  the Ma'ayan Lab (for human and mouse samples - see  ARCHS4 ) and by NCBI staff (for human samples;  explanation ,  example ). Although the samples in this experiment were human cell lines, it appears that neither set of results was generated, presumably as a consequence of an unusual choice mentioned in the article: 
 
 The data generated in this study are publicly available at Sequence Read Archive with Accession: PRJNA822231. 
 
 This data was not associated with a GEO series. In addition, the reads are no longer in  SRA , but the associated records are: 
 
 No public data is linked to this project.(...) 
 
   
 
 
 
 Title 
 
 KDM6A Loss Triggers an Epigenetic Switch That Disrupts Urothelial Differentiation and Drives Cell Proliferation in Bladder Cancer 
 
 
 
 Authors 
 Hong Qiu, Vladimir Makarov, Jennifer K Bolzenius, Angela Halstead, Yvonne Parker, Allen Wang, Gopakumar V Iyer, Hannah Wise, Daniel Kim, Varna Thayaparan, Daniel J Lindner, Georges-Pascal Haber, Angela H Ting, Bing Ren, Timothy A Chan, Vivek Arora, David B Solit, Byron H Lee 
 
 
 Journal & issue 
 Cancer Res. 2023 Jan 13;83(6):814–829 
 
 
 DOI 
 10.1158/0008-5472.CAN-22-1444 
 
 
 Pubmed 
  36638328  
 
 
 RNA-seq samples 
 human cell lines RT4, SCaBER, SW780
